title: ulmus glabra f. lutescens
intro: Ulmus glabra f. lutescens, also known as the Scotch Elm or Wych Elm, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe and western Asia. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its bark, which contains a compound called salicin, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent that is also found in willow bark. The health benefits of Ulmus glabra f. lutescens include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, the bark is often made into a decoction or tincture, which can be used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
